323	def demo_test():
324	    from transformers import AutoConfig, AutoTokenizer
325	
326	    from sglang.test.test_utils import DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST
327	
328	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
329	    hf_config = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(DEFAULT_MODEL_NAME_FOR_TEST)
330	
331	    # Should use vocab size from model config
332	    vocab_size = hf_config.vocab_size
333	    eos_token_id = tokenizer.eos_token_id
334	
335	    backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
336	        tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, model_eos_token_ids=[eos_token_id]
337	    )
338	    regex = r"hello (world|there)"
339	    grammar = backend.dispatch_regex(regex)
340	    tokens = [
341	        tokenizer.encode(t, add_special_tokens=False)[0] for t in ["hello", " world"]
342	    ]
343	
344	    # Test termination
345	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[0])  # accept "hello"
346	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[1])  # accept " world"
347	    grammar.accept_token(eos_token_id)  # accept EOS
348	    assert grammar.is_terminated()
349	
350	    # Test rollback the terminated state
351	    grammar.rollback(1)
352	    assert not grammar.is_terminated()
353	
354	
355	if __name__ == "__main__":
356	    demo_test()

42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state

114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
115	    def __init__(
116	        self,
117	        tokenizer,
118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
151	                # outlines <= 0.0.46
152	                guide = RegexGuide(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
153	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
154	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
155	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
156	
157	        jump_forward_map = None
158	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)
159	
160	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str):
161	        return super().dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
162	
163	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str):
164	        return super().dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
165	
166	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
167	        try:
168	            regex = build_regex_from_object(
169	                key_string,
170	                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
171	            )
172	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
173	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
174	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
175	        return self._compile_regex(regex)
176	
177	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
178	        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
179	
180	
181	def build_regex_from_object(
182	    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
183	):
184	    if isinstance(object, type(BaseModel)):
185	        schema = json.dumps(object.model_json_schema())
186	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
187	        schema = json.dumps(object)
188	    else:
189	        schema = object
190	    return build_regex_from_schema(schema, whitespace_pattern)

244	    max_tokens = 64

247	    @sgl.function
248	    def parallel_decoding(s, topic):
249	        s += "Act as a helpful assistant.\n"

252	            "ASSISTANT: Okay. Here are "

74	    def __init__(self, tools: List[Tool], tool_call_parser: str):
75	        detector_class = self.ToolCallParserEnum.get(tool_call_parser)
76	        if detector_class:
77	            detector = detector_class()
78	        else:
79	            raise ValueError(f"Unsupported tool_call_parser: {tool_call_parser}")
80	
81	        self.detector = detector
82	        self.tools = tools
83	        self.tool_strict_level = envs.SGLANG_TOOL_STRICT_LEVEL.get()
84	
85	    def has_tool_call(self, text: str) -> bool:
86	        """
87	        Check if the given text contains a tool call in the format supported by this parser.
88	        This delegates to the detector's implementation.
89	
90	        Args:
91	            text: The text to check for tool calls
92	
93	        Returns:
94	            True if the text contains a tool call, False otherwise
95	        """
96	        if not self.tools:
97	            return False
98	        return self.detector.has_tool_call(text)
99	
100	    def parse_non_stream(self, full_text: str) -> Tuple[str, list[ToolCallItem]]:
101	        """
102	        One-time parsing of the full text to extract tool calls.
103	
104	        Args:
105	            full_text: The complete text to parse
106	
107	        Returns:
108	            A tuple containing:
109	            - The remaining text after parsing that was not consumed by the detector (can be treated as normal text)
110	            - A list of tool calls parsed from the text
111	        """
112	        if not self.tools:
113	            return full_text, []
114	        parsed_result = self.detector.detect_and_parse(full_text, self.tools)
115	        tool_call_list = parsed_result.calls
116	        if tool_call_list:
117	            return parsed_result.normal_text, tool_call_list
118	        else:
119	            return full_text, []
120	
121	    def parse_stream_chunk(self, chunk_text: str) -> Tuple[str, list[ToolCallItem]]:
122	        """
123	        Streaming incremental parsing of chunks of text as they arrive.
124	
125	        Args:
126	            chunk_text: The new chunk of text to parse
127	
128	        Returns:
129	            A tuple containing:
130	            - The normal text that should be displayed to the user
131	            - A list of tool calls parsed from the chunk
132	        """
133	        if not self.tools:
134	            return chunk_text, []
135	        final_normal_text = ""
136	        final_calls = []
137	
138	        sp_result = self.detector.parse_streaming_increment(chunk_text, self.tools)
139	        if sp_result.normal_text:
140	            final_normal_text = sp_result.normal_text
141	        if sp_result.calls:
142	            final_calls.extend(sp_result.calls)
143	            final_normal_text = sp_result.normal_text
144	
145	        return final_normal_text, final_calls

21	@dataclasses.dataclass
22	class SamplingBatchInfo:
23	    # Basic batched sampling params
24	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
25	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
26	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
27	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
28	
29	    # Whether all requests use greedy sampling
30	    is_all_greedy: bool
31	
32	    # Whether any requests use top_p sampling

38	    # Whether any request needs min_p sampling

45	    apply_mask_func: Optional[Callable[[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor], None]] = None

50	
51	    # Whether any request has custom logit processor

54	    custom_params: Optional[List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]] = None
55	    # Custom logit processor
56	    custom_logit_processor: Optional[
57	        Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]
58	    ] = None
59	
60	    # Used for deterministic sampling
61	    sampling_seed: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
62	
63	    # Device
64	    device: str = "cuda"
65	
66	    # Handle logit bias
67	    logit_bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None

69	    @classmethod
70	    def from_schedule_batch(cls, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
71	        global_server_args = get_global_server_args()
72	        enable_deterministic = global_server_args.enable_deterministic_inference
73	
74	        reqs = batch.reqs
75	        device = batch.device
76	        temperatures = torch.tensor(
77	            [r.sampling_params.temperature for r in reqs],
78	            dtype=torch.float,
79	            device=device,
80	        ).view(-1, 1)
81	        top_ps = torch.tensor(
82	            [r.sampling_params.top_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
83	        )
84	        top_ks = torch.tensor(
85	            [r.sampling_params.top_k for r in reqs], dtype=torch.int32, device=device
86	        )
87	        min_ps = torch.tensor(
88	            [r.sampling_params.min_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
89	        )
90	        sampling_seed = (
91	            torch.tensor(
92	                [
93	                    (
94	                        r.sampling_params.sampling_seed
95	                        if r.sampling_params.sampling_seed is not None
96	                        else 42
97	                    )
98	                    for r in reqs
99	                ],
100	                dtype=torch.int64,
101	                device=device,
102	            )
103	            if enable_deterministic
104	            else None
105	        )
106	
107	        logit_bias = None
108	        if any(r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None for r in reqs):
109	            logit_bias = torch.zeros(len(reqs), vocab_size, device=device)
110	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
111	                if r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
112	                    for key, value in r.sampling_params.logit_bias.items():
113	                        logit_bias[i, int(key)] = value
114	
115	        # Check if any request has custom logit processor
116	        has_custom_logit_processor = (
117	            global_server_args.enable_custom_logit_processor
118	            and any(r.custom_logit_processor for r in reqs)  # check the flag first.
119	        )  # then check the requests.
120	
121	        if has_custom_logit_processor:
122	            # Merge the same type of custom logit processors together
123	            processor_dict = {}
124	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
125	                if r.custom_logit_processor is None:
126	                    continue
127	                processor_str = r.custom_logit_processor
128	                if processor_str not in processor_dict:
129	                    processor_dict[processor_str] = []
130	                processor_dict[processor_str].append(i)
131	
132	            merged_custom_logit_processor = {
133	                hash(processor_str): (
134	                    # The deserialized custom logit processor object
135	                    CustomLogitProcessor.from_str(processor_str),
136	                    # The mask tensor for the requests that use this custom logit processor
137	                    torch.zeros(len(reqs), dtype=torch.bool)
138	                    .scatter_(0, torch.tensor(true_indices), True)
139	                    .to(device, non_blocking=True),
140	                )
141	                for processor_str, true_indices in processor_dict.items()
142	            }
143	            custom_params = [r.sampling_params.custom_params for r in reqs]
144	        else:
145	            merged_custom_logit_processor = None
146	            custom_params = None
147	
148	        # Each penalizers will do nothing if they evaluate themselves as not required by looking at
149	        # the sampling_params of the requests (See {_is_required()} of each penalizers). So this
150	        # should not add hefty computation overhead other than simple checks.
151	        #
152	        # While we can choose not to even create the class instances if they are not required, this
153	        # could add additional complexity to the {ScheduleBatch} class, especially we need to
154	        # handle {filter_batch()} and {merge_batch()} cases as well.
155	        penalizer_orchestrator = penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator(
156	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
157	            batch=batch,
158	            penalizers={
159	                penaltylib.BatchedFrequencyPenalizer,
160	                penaltylib.BatchedMinNewTokensPenalizer,
161	                penaltylib.BatchedPresencePenalizer,
162	            },
163	        )
164	
165	        ret = cls(
166	            temperatures=temperatures,
167	            top_ps=top_ps,
168	            top_ks=top_ks,
169	            min_ps=min_ps,
170	            sampling_seed=sampling_seed,
171	            is_all_greedy=all(r.sampling_params.top_k <= 1 for r in reqs),
172	            need_top_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_p != 1.0 for r in reqs),
173	            need_top_k_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_k != TOP_K_ALL for r in reqs),
174	            need_min_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.min_p > 0 for r in reqs),
175	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
176	            penalizer_orchestrator=penalizer_orchestrator,
177	            has_custom_logit_processor=has_custom_logit_processor,
178	            custom_params=custom_params,
179	            custom_logit_processor=merged_custom_logit_processor,
180	            device=device,
181	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
182	        )
183	        ret.adjusted_from_schedule_batch(batch, vocab_size)
184	        return ret
185

224	    def update_penalties(self):
225	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
226	            self.acc_linear_penalties = torch.zeros(
227	                (len(self.temperatures), self.vocab_size),

235	    def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):

241	            # Used in the non-overlap mode

244	        if self.vocab_mask is not None:

247	        if self.logit_bias is not None:

2	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
3	
4	import openai
5	import requests
6	
7	
8	class TestJSONConstrainedMixin:
9	    json_schema = json.dumps(
10	        {
11	            "type": "object",
12	            "properties": {
13	                "name": {"type": "string", "pattern": "^[\\w]+$"},
14	                "population": {"type": "integer"},
15	            },
16	            "required": ["name", "population"],
17	            "additionalProperties": False,
18	        }
19	    )
20	
21	    def _run_decode_json(
22	        self, json_schema, return_logprob=False, top_logprobs_num=0, n=1
23	    ):
24	        response = requests.post(
25	            self.base_url + "/generate",
26	            json={
27	                "text": (
28	                    "Introduce the capital of France. Return in a JSON format. The JSON Schema is: "
29	                    + json.dumps(json_schema)
30	                ),
31	                "sampling_params": {
32	                    "temperature": 0 if n == 1 else 0.5,
33	                    "max_new_tokens": 128,
34	                    "n": n,
35	                    "stop_token_ids": [119690],
36	                    "json_schema": json_schema,
37	                },
38	                "stream": False,
39	                "return_logprob": return_logprob,
40	                "top_logprobs_num": top_logprobs_num,
41	                "logprob_start_len": 0,
42	            },
43	        )
44	        ret = response.json()
45	        print(json.dumps(ret))
46	        print("=" * 100)
47	
48	        if not json_schema or json_schema == "INVALID":
49	            return
50	
51	        # Make sure the json output is valid
52	        try:
53	            js_obj = json.loads(ret["text"])
54	        except (TypeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError):
55	            raise
56	
57	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["name"], str)
58	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["population"], int)
59	
60	    def test_json_generate(self):
61	        self._run_decode_json(json_schema=self.json_schema)
62	
63	    def test_json_invalid(self):
64	        self._run_decode_json(json_schema="INVALID")
65	
66	    def test_json_openai(self):
67	        client = openai.Client(api_key="EMPTY", base_url=f"{self.base_url}/v1")
68	
69	        response = client.chat.completions.create(
70	            model=self.model,
71	            messages=[
72	                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful AI assistant"},
73	                {
74	                    "role": "user",
75	                    "content": "Introduce the capital of France. Return in a JSON format. "
76	                    "The JSON Schema is: " + json.dumps(self.json_schema),
77	                },
78	            ],
79	            temperature=0,
80	            max_tokens=128,
81	            response_format={
82	                "type": "json_schema",
83	                "json_schema": {"name": "foo", "schema": json.loads(self.json_schema)},
84	            },
85	        )
86	        text = response.choices[0].message.content
87	
88	        try:
89	            js_obj = json.loads(text)
90	        except (TypeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError):
91	            print("JSONDecodeError", text)
92	            raise
93	
94	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["name"], str)
95	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["population"], int)
96	
97	    def test_mix_json_and_other(self):
98	        json_schemas = [None, None, self.json_schema, self.json_schema] * 10
99	
100	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(len(json_schemas)) as executor:
101	            list(executor.map(self._run_decode_json, json_schemas))

775	    def _resolve_sampling_params(self, sampling_params):
776	        """
777	        Construct sampling param based on default + override values
778	
779	        The default values of sampling are populated in `default_sampling_para` via sgl.function.run(...sampling_args)
780	        , and `sampling_params` contains the override values from sgl.gen().
781	
782	        Here we use default_sampling_para as the base and override the values if they exist in `sampling_params`.
783	        It also extends the stop tokens based on the chat template.
784	        """
785	
786	        # deepcopy is required because the dict has lists inside
787	        clone = copy.deepcopy(self.default_sampling_para)
788	
789	        for item in [
790	            "max_new_tokens",
791	            "min_new_tokens",
792	            "n",
793	            "stop",
794	            "stop_token_ids",
795	            "stop_regex",
796	            "temperature",
797	            "top_p",

811	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)

821	
822	        return clone

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a final report with all the findings.

## SGLang Programming API Structure - Comprehensive Report

I've thoroughly explored the SGLang codebase and identified all the key components for the user-facing API and constraint handling. Here's what I found:

### 1. **Main User-Facing API** (`/python/sglang/__init__.py` and `/python/sglang/lang/api.py`)

**Primary Public APIs Exported:**
- `gen()` - Core generation function
- `gen_int()` - Type-constrained integer generation
- `gen_string()` - Type-constrained string generation
- `select()` - Choice-based generation
- `system()`, `user()`, `assistant()` - Role/message builders
- `system_begin()`, `system_end()`, `user_begin()`, `user_end()`, `assistant_begin()`, `assistant_end()` - Role delimiters
- `function()` - Decorator for SGLang functions
- `image()`, `video()` - Media support
- `separate_reasoning()` - Extended thinking support
- `flush_cache()`, `get_server_info()`, `set_default_backend()` - Backend utilities

### 2. **Generation Functions and Constraint Parameters**

**File:** `/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (lines 75-139, 142-225, 236-243)

**Core `gen()` Function Signature:**
```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
)
```

**Key Constraint Parameters:**
- **`regex`** (str): Regular expression constraint for output format
- **`json_schema`** (str): JSON schema constraint for structured output
- **`choices`** (List[str]): List of valid choices (creates SglSelect internally)
- **`choices_method`** (ChoicesSamplingMethod): How to sample from choices (defaults to `token_length_normalized`)
- **`dtype`** (type or str): Type constraint (int, str, or custom type)

**select() Function Signature:**
```python
def select(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temperature: float = 0.0,
    choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod = token_length_normalized,
)
```

### 3. **Intermediate Representation (IR) Classes**

**File:** `/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` (lines 451-549)

**SglSamplingParams Dataclass** (lines 18-62):
- Stores all sampling configuration including `regex` and `json_schema` as constraint fields
- Has `to_*_kwargs()` methods to convert to backend-specific formats

**SglGen Class** (lines 451-504):
```python
class SglGen(SglExpr):
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        # ... sampling parameters ...
        dtype: Optional[type] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
            # ... all params ...
            dtype=dtype,
            regex=regex,
            json_schema=json_schema,
        )
```

**SglSelect Class** (lines 533-549):
```python
class SglSelect(SglExpr):
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: str,
        choices: List[str],
        temperature: float,
        choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod,
    ):
        # Choice constraint handling
```

### 4. **Constraint Implementation Details**

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py` (lines 207-246)

**JSON Schema Constraint Function:**
```python
def get_json_schema_constraint(
    tools: List[Tool], 
    tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["required"]]
) -> Optional[dict]:
```
- Generates JSON schema constraints for structured generation
- Supports tool choice specifications ("required", specific tool selection, "auto", None)
- Consolidates `$defs` across multiple tools with conflict detection

### 5. **Real-World Usage Examples**

**File:** `/benchmark/json_decode_regex/bench_sglang.py` (lines 16-28, 31-44)
```python
@sgl.function
def json_decode(s, document):
    s += "..."
    with s.var_scope("json_output"):
        s += "{\n"
        s += '  "name": ' + sgl.gen("name", max_tokens=8, regex=REGEX_STR + ",") + "\n"
        s += '  "population": ' + sgl.gen("population", max_tokens=8, regex=REGEX_INT + ",") + "\n"
        # ... more fields with regex constraints
```

**File:** `/benchmark/json_schema/bench_sglang.py` (lines 19-26)
```python
@sgl.function
def schema_gen(s, message: Tuple[str, str], json_schema: str):
    system, user = message
    s += sgl.system(system)
    s += sgl.user(user)
    s += sgl.assistant(
        sgl.gen("json_output", temperature=0, max_tokens=256, json_schema=json_schema)
    )
```

**File:** `/benchmark/hellaswag/bench_sglang.py` (lines 61-64)
```python
@sgl.function

    s += few_shot_examples + question

```

**File:** `/examples/frontend_language/usage/readme_examples.py`
```python
@sgl.function
def tool_use(s, question):
    s += "To answer this question: " + question + ". "
    s += "I need to use a " + sgl.gen("tool", choices=["calculator", "search engine"]) + ". "
    # ... choice-based generation

@sgl.function
def regular_expression_gen(s):
    s += "Q: What is the IP address of the Google DNS servers?\n"
    s += "A: " + sgl.gen(
        "answer",
        temperature=0,
        regex=r"((25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|[01]?\d\d?).){3}(25[0-5]|2[0-4]\d|[01]?\d\d?)",
    )
```

### 6. **Test Examples Showing Constraint Usage**

**File:** `/python/sglang/test/test_programs.py`

- **test_select()** (lines 65-96): Demonstrates choice constraint with `sgl.select("answer", ["True", "False", "Unknown"])`
- **test_decode_int()** (lines 99-107): Shows `sgl.gen_int()` for type-constrained integer generation
- **test_decode_json_regex()** (lines 110-136): Shows regex constraints for JSON field generation
- **test_decode_json()** (lines 138-159): Shows dtype constraints (int, str) for structured generation
- **test_react()** (lines 207-240): Shows `sgl.select()` for constrained generation in ReAct-style programs

**File:** `/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py`

Comprehensive test suite showing:
- JSON schema constraint generation for tool calls
- Handling of `$defs` and type references
- Schema consolidation and conflict detection
- Support for "required", specific tool selection, "auto", and None choices

### 7. **Backend Support and Constraint Passing**

The constraint parameters flow through the system:
1. User calls `gen(regex=..., json_schema=...)` or `select(choices=...)`
2. Creates `SglGen` or `SglSelect` IR nodes with sampling params
3. IR nodes are interpreted/executed by the backend
4. Backend converts constraints to its native format via `to_*_kwargs()` methods

### 8. **Export Structure**

**Public API Export Hierarchy:**
- `sglang/__init__.py` → imports from `sglang.lang.api`
- `sglang.lang.api` → defines all generation and constraint functions
- `sglang.lang.ir` → defines intermediate representation classes
- `sglang.lang.backend.*` → backend implementations (OpenAI, RuntimeEndpoint, etc.)
- `sglang.lang.choices` → choice sampling methods
- `sglang.srt.function_call.utils` → constraint utility functions

### Summary

SGLang provides three primary constraint mechanisms through the `gen()` function:
1. **Regex constraints** (`regex` parameter) - Pattern-based output format control
2. **JSON Schema constraints** (`json_schema` parameter) - Structured JSON output validation
3. **Choice constraints** (`choices` parameter or `select()` function) - Discrete selection from predefined options

All constraints are passed through the intermediate representation layer and ultimately handled by the backend infrastructure for enforcement during generation.

## SGLang Programming API Structure - Comprehensive Report

I've thoroughly explored the SGLang codebase and identified all the key components for the user-facing API and constraint handling. Here's what I found:

### 1. **Main User-Facing API** (`/python/sglang/__init__.py` and `/python/sglang/lang/api.py`)

**Primary Public APIs Exported:**
- `gen()` - Core generation function
- `gen_int()` - Type-constrained integer generation
- `gen_string()` - Type-constrained string generation
- `select()` - Choice-based generation
- `system()`, `user()`, `assistant()` - Role/message builders
- `system_begin()`, `system_end()`, `user_begin()`, `user_end()`, `assistant_begin()`, `assistant_end()` - Role delimiters
- `function()` - Decorator for SGLang functions
- `image()`, `video()` - Media support
- `separate_reasoning()` - Extended thinking support
- `flush_cache()`, `get_server_info()`, `set_default_backend()` - Backend utilities

### 2. **Generation Functions and Constraint Parameters**

**File:** `/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (lines 75-139, 142-225, 236-243)

**Core `gen()` Function Signature:**
```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
)
```

**Key Constraint Parameters:**
- **`regex`** (str): Regular expression constraint for output format
- **`json_schema`** (str): JSON schema constraint for structured output
- **`choices`** (List[str]): List of valid choices (creates SglSelect internally)
- **`choices_method`** (ChoicesSamplingMethod): How to sample from choices (defaults to `token_length_normalized`)
- **`dtype`** (type or str): Type constraint (int, str, or custom type)

**select() Function Signature:**
```python
def select(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temperature: float = 0.0,
    choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod = token_length_normalized,
)
```

### 3. **Intermediate Representation (IR) Classes**

**File:** `/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` (lines 451-549)

**SglSamplingParams Dataclass** (lines 18-62):
- Stores all sampling configuration including `regex` and `json_schema` as constraint fields
- Has `to_*_kwargs()` methods to convert to backend-specific formats

**SglGen Class** (lines 451-504):
```python
class SglGen(SglExpr):
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        # ... sampling parameters ...
        dtype: Optional[type] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
            # ... all params ...
            dtype=dtype,
            regex=regex,
            json_schema=json_schema,
        )
```

**SglSelect Class** (lines 533-549):
```python
class SglSelect(SglExpr):
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: str,
        choices: List[str],
        temperature: float,
        choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod,
    ):
        # Choice constraint handling
```

### 4. **Constraint Implementation Details**

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py` (lines 207-246)

**JSON Schema Constraint Function:**

def get_json_schema_constraint(
    tools: List[Tool], 
    tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["required"]]

```
- Generates JSON schema constraints for structured generation
- Supports tool choice specifications ("required", specific tool selection, "auto", None)
- Consolidates `$defs` across multiple tools with conflict detection

### 5. **Real-World Usage Examples**

**File:** `/benchmark/json_decode_regex/bench_sglang.py` (lines 16-28, 31-44)

        s += '  "population": ' + sgl.gen("population", max_tokens=8, regex=REGEX_INT + ",") + "\n"

```

    s += sgl.user(user)

        sgl.gen("json_output", temperature=0, max_tokens=256, json_schema=json_schema)

**File:** `/benchmark/hellaswag/bench_sglang.py` (lines 61-64)

- **test_select()** (lines 65-96): Demonstrates choice constraint with `sgl.select("answer", ["True", "False", "Unknown"])`
- **test_decode_int()** (lines 99-107): Shows `sgl.gen_int()` for type-constrained integer generation

**File:** `/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py`

Comprehensive test suite showing:
- JSON schema constraint generation for tool calls
- Handling of `$defs` and type references
- Schema consolidation and conflict detection
- Support for "required", specific tool selection, "auto", and None choices

### 7. **Backend Support and Constraint Passing**

The constraint parameters flow through the system:
1. User calls `gen(regex=..., json_schema=...)` or `select(choices=...)`
2. Creates `SglGen` or `SglSelect` IR nodes with sampling params
3. IR nodes are interpreted/executed by the backend
4. Backend converts constraints to its native format via `to_*_kwargs()` methods

### 8. **Export Structure**

**Public API Export Hierarchy:**
- `sglang/__init__.py` → imports from `sglang.lang.api`
- `sglang.lang.api` → defines all generation and constraint functions
- `sglang.lang.ir` → defines intermediate representation classes
- `sglang.lang.backend.*` → backend implementations (OpenAI, RuntimeEndpoint, etc.)
- `sglang.lang.choices` → choice sampling methods
- `sglang.srt.function_call.utils` → constraint utility functions

### Summary

SGLang provides three primary constraint mechanisms through the `gen()` function:
1. **Regex constraints** (`regex` parameter) - Pattern-based output format control
2. **JSON Schema constraints** (`json_schema` parameter) - Structured JSON output validation

All constraints are passed through the intermediate representation layer and ultimately handled by the backend infrastructure for enforcement during generation.

14	"""The baseclass of a backend for reasoner grammar-guided constrained decoding."""
15	
16	from typing import List, Optional, Tuple
17	
18	import torch
19	
20	from .base_grammar_backend import (
21	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
22	    BaseGrammarBackend,
23	    BaseGrammarObject,
24	)
25	
26	
27	class ReasonerGrammarObject(BaseGrammarObject):
28	    def __init__(self, grammar: BaseGrammarObject, think_end_id: int):
29	        super().__init__()
30	        self.grammar = grammar
31	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
32	        # -1    means thinking has not ended yet
33	        # 0     means just ended thinking in the last token
34	        # +     means number of tokens after thinking ended
35	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
36	
37	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
38	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1 if reasoning else 0
39	
40	    def transfer_state(self, token: int) -> int:
41	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == -1 and token == self.think_end_id:
42	            self.tokens_after_think_end = 0
43	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
44	            self.tokens_after_think_end += 1
45	
46	    def rollback_state(self):
47	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == 0:
48	            self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
49	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end > 0:
50	            self.tokens_after_think_end -= 1
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
53	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
54	            self.grammar.accept_token(token)
55	        self.transfer_state(token)
56	
57	    def is_terminated(self):
58	        return self.grammar.is_terminated()
59	
60	    def rollback(self, k):
61	        steps_after_think = min(k, self.tokens_after_think_end)
62	        if steps_after_think > 0:
63	            self.grammar.rollback(steps_after_think)
64	
65	        for _ in range(k):
66	            self.rollback_state()
67	
68	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
69	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
70	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
71	        return self.grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device)
72	
73	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
74	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
75	            self.grammar.fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, idx)
76	
77	    def move_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
78	        return self.grammar.move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, device)
79	
80	    @property
81	    def apply_vocab_mask(self):
82	        return self.grammar.apply_vocab_mask
83	
84	    def copy(self) -> BaseGrammarObject:
85	        return ReasonerGrammarObject(self.grammar.copy(), self.think_end_id)
86	
87	    @property
88	    def finished(self):
89	        return self.grammar.finished
90	
91	    @finished.setter
92	    def finished(self, finished):
93	        self.grammar.finished = finished
94	
95	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer):
96	        return self.grammar.try_jump_forward(tokenizer)
97	
98	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper):
99	        return self.grammar.jump_forward_str_state(helper)
100	
101	    def jump_and_retokenize(
102	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
103	    ):
104	        return self.grammar.jump_and_retokenize(
105	            old_output_ids, new_output_ids, next_state
106	        )
107	
108	
109	class ReasonerGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	    def __init__(self, grammar_backend: BaseGrammarBackend, think_end_id):
111	        super().__init__()
112	        self.grammar_backend = grammar_backend
113	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
114	
115	    def _init_value_dispatch(
116	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], reasoning: bool
117	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
118	        ret = self.grammar_backend._init_value_dispatch(key, reasoning)
119	        # avoid wrapping invalid grammar, so that the scheduler can detect it
120	        if ret is None or ret is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
121	            return ret
122	        obj = ReasonerGrammarObject(ret, self.think_end_id)
123	        obj.maybe_init_reasoning(reasoning)
124	        return obj

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document:

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a comprehensive report on how output constraints are implemented:

## SGLang Output Constraints Implementation

### 1. High-Level Architecture

Output constraints in SGLang follow a **backend-abstraction pattern** where constraints are compiled to state machines that guide token selection during generation. The system supports three primary constraint types:

1. **Regex** - Regular expression patterns
2. **JSON Schema** - JSON structure constraints  
3. **EBNF** - Extended Backus-Naur Form grammars

Additionally, there are specialized types:
- **Structural Tags** - XML-like tagged output structures
- **Choices** - Token-level selection from discrete options

### 2. Core File Structure

**Location:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/`

**Key Files:**
- **`base_grammar_backend.py`** - Abstract base classes and registration system
- **`grammar_manager.py`** - Request queue and grammar compilation management
- **`xgrammar_backend.py`** - XGrammar backend (primary production backend)
- **`outlines_backend.py`** - Outlines library backend
- **`llguidance_backend.py`** - LLGuidance backend
- **`reasoner_grammar_backend.py`** - Wrapper for reasoning models

### 3. Base Classes & Interfaces

**`BaseGrammarObject`** (base_grammar_backend.py, lines 42-118):
```
Abstract base class for constraint state machines
Key methods:
- accept_token(token: int) → None
  Accept next token and advance state
- fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: Tensor, idx: int) → None  
  Mark allowed tokens in logit mask
- apply_vocab_mask(logits: Tensor, vocab_mask: Tensor) → None
  Mask logits using vocab mask
- try_jump_forward(tokenizer) → Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]
  Optimization: skip multiple tokens when possible
- is_terminated() → bool
  Check if constraint is satisfied
- copy() → BaseGrammarObject
  Create independent copy for parallel sequences
```

**`BaseGrammarBackend`** (base_grammar_backend.py, lines 129-274):
```
Dispatches constraint types to appropriate implementations
Key dispatch methods:
- dispatch_json(key_string: str) → BaseGrammarObject
- dispatch_regex(key_string: str) → BaseGrammarObject  
- dispatch_ebnf(key_string: str) → BaseGrammarObject
- dispatch_structural_tag(key_string: str) → BaseGrammarObject

Caching:
- get_cached_or_future_value(key: Tuple[str, str]) → BaseGrammarObject
  Lazy compilation with ThreadPoolExecutor, returns Future if not cached
```

### 4. User-Facing API

**Location:** `/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (lines 75-139)

```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,           # Constraint parameter
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,     # Constraint parameter
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,   # Special case (routing to SglSelect)
    # ... other sampling parameters
):
```

Also in `/python/sglang/lang/ir.py`:
- **`SglGen`** class (lines 451-504) - IR node for generation with constraints
- **`SglSamplingParams`** dataclass (lines 18-41) - Stores regex and json_schema

### 5. Constraint Registration & Creation

**Registration system** (base_grammar_backend.py, lines 199-218):
```python
GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}

def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func

def create_grammar_backend(server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids):
    # Routes to appropriate backend (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance, or none)
    # Also wraps with ReasonerGrammarBackend if reasoning mode enabled
```

**Flow:**
1. User specifies `--grammar-backend` flag (e.g., "xgrammar")
2. Server initializes grammar backend in `GrammarManager.__init__`
3. Requests with constraints go through `GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar`
4. Backend dispatches based on constraint type (json/regex/ebnf/structural_tag)

### 6. Constraint Integration in Generation Pipeline

**Location:** `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py` (lines 197-249)

**Batched constraint handling:**
```python
def update_regex_vocab_mask():
    # Create shared vocab mask (all grammars allocate same size)
    vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(...)
    
    # Fill mask for each request's current state
    for i, grammar in enumerate(grammars):
        if grammar and not grammar.finished:
            grammar.fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, i)
    
def apply_logits_bias(logits: Tensor):
    if vocab_mask is not None:
        # Apply constraint mask to logits BEFORE sampling
        apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=vocab_mask)
```

**Token acceptance flow:**
1. Token sampled from masked logits
2. Grammar accepts token: `grammar.accept_token(token_id)`
3. Grammar state advances to next state
4. Next iteration: new mask generated from updated state

### 7. Backend Implementations

**XGrammar Backend** (xgrammar_backend.py):
- Uses `xgrammar` library (C++/Rust implementation)
- Compiles to NFA/DFA
- Fast token masking with bitmasks
- Device-aware (CUDA/CPU/NPU/XPU)

Key class: **`XGrammarGrammar`** (lines 53-167)
```python
def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask, idx):
    self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)
    
def apply_vocab_mask(self, logits, vocab_mask):
    apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, vocab_mask)
```

**Outlines Backend** (outlines_backend.py):
- Uses `outlines` library FSM
- Built on interegular for regex compilation
- Slower than XGrammar but supports more backends

Key class: **`OutlinesGrammar`** (lines 42-112)

**LLGuidance Backend** (llguidance_backend.py):
- Uses `llguidance` library
- Supports structural tags with custom XML-like syntax
- Separate token bitmask allocation

### 8. Constraint Request Flow

**`GrammarManager`** (grammar_manager.py):

1. **`process_req_with_grammar(req)`** (lines 67-105):
   - Detects constraint type in sampling_params
   - Creates cache key: `(constraint_type, constraint_string)`
   - Gets grammar object (cached or future)
   - Adds to grammar_queue if compilation needed

2. **`get_ready_grammar_requests()`** (lines 107-195):
   - Polls for completed grammar compilations
   - Synchronizes across distributed ranks
   - Handles timeouts
   - Merges ready requests back to main scheduler

### 9. Constraint Type Handling

**JSON Schema:**
- Converted to regex via backend (e.g., outlines' `build_regex_from_schema`)
- Both XGrammar and Outlines support JSON directly: `compile_json_schema()`

**Regex:**
- Direct compilation: `compile_regex(pattern_string)`
- State machine guides valid next tokens

**EBNF:**
- Grammar rule compilation: `compile_grammar(ebnf_string)`
- Full grammar syntax support

**Structural Tags (Legacy):**
- XML-like: `<begin>schema</begin>...<end>schema</end>`
- Allows multiple labeled sections with schemas
- Wrapped in JSON trigger structure

**Choices (Special):**
- Not grammar-based; routed to `SglSelect` (ir.py lines 533-549)
- Uses logprob-based selection: `ChoicesSamplingMethod` (choices.py)
- Methods: token_length_normalized, greedy_token_selection, unconditional_likelihood_normalized

### 10. Constraint Validation

**Sampling parameters validation** (sampling_params.py, lines 155-161):
```python
grammars = [self.json_schema, self.regex, self.ebnf]
if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
    raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
```

Mutual exclusivity enforced: only one constraint type per generation request.

### 11. State Management

**Grammar state includes:**
- Current FSM state
- Accepted tokens list (for debugging)
- Finished flag (constraint satisfied)
- Grammar stats (compilation time, cache hit)
- Rollback capability (for speculative decoding)

**Per-request state tracking:**
- Each request has independent grammar object copy
- Parallel generation tracks separate states
- Speculative decoding can rollback N tokens

### 12. Performance Optimizations

1. **Caching:** Compiled grammars cached by (type, string) key
2. **Jump-forward:** Skip single tokens for known sequences
3. **Batch masking:** Single vocab_mask tensor for entire batch
4. **Lazy compilation:** Threaded compilation doesn't block scheduling
5. **Copy-on-use:** Grammar objects copied only when needed (parallel generation)

### 13. Test Files

Key test/example files:
- `/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py` - JSON schema constraint tests
- `/python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py` - Integration test mixin
- `/python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py` - Regex constraint tests
- `/python/sglang/test/kits/ebnf_constrained_kit.py` - EBNF constraint tests

### Summary

SGLang's constraint system is a **pluggable, backend-agnostic architecture** where:
1. Constraints are compiled to state machines by swappable backends
2. Generated tokens are masked based on current constraint state
3. Request-level parallelism supported through state copying
4. Compilation is lazy and cached for performance
5. Reasoning models get wrapped constraint support automatically
6. Clear separation between API layer (gen/select), IR (SglGen/SglSelect), and runtime (GrammarManager/GrammarBackend)

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document:

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a comprehensive report on how output constraints are implemented:

## SGLang Output Constraints Implementation

### 1. High-Level Architecture

Output constraints in SGLang follow a **backend-abstraction pattern** where constraints are compiled to state machines that guide token selection during generation. The system supports three primary constraint types:

1. **Regex** - Regular expression patterns
2. **JSON Schema** - JSON structure constraints  
3. **EBNF** - Extended Backus-Naur Form grammars

Additionally, there are specialized types:
- **Structural Tags** - XML-like tagged output structures
- **Choices** - Token-level selection from discrete options

### 2. Core File Structure

**Location:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/`

**Key Files:**
- **`base_grammar_backend.py`** - Abstract base classes and registration system
- **`grammar_manager.py`** - Request queue and grammar compilation management
- **`xgrammar_backend.py`** - XGrammar backend (primary production backend)
- **`outlines_backend.py`** - Outlines library backend
- **`llguidance_backend.py`** - LLGuidance backend
- **`reasoner_grammar_backend.py`** - Wrapper for reasoning models

### 3. Base Classes & Interfaces

**`BaseGrammarObject`** (base_grammar_backend.py, lines 42-118):
```
Abstract base class for constraint state machines
Key methods:
- accept_token(token: int) → None
  Accept next token and advance state
- fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: Tensor, idx: int) → None  
  Mark allowed tokens in logit mask
- apply_vocab_mask(logits: Tensor, vocab_mask: Tensor) → None
  Mask logits using vocab mask
- try_jump_forward(tokenizer) → Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]
  Optimization: skip multiple tokens when possible
- is_terminated() → bool
  Check if constraint is satisfied
- copy() → BaseGrammarObject
  Create independent copy for parallel sequences
```

**`BaseGrammarBackend`** (base_grammar_backend.py, lines 129-274):
```
Dispatches constraint types to appropriate implementations
Key dispatch methods:
- dispatch_json(key_string: str) → BaseGrammarObject
- dispatch_regex(key_string: str) → BaseGrammarObject  
- dispatch_ebnf(key_string: str) → BaseGrammarObject
- dispatch_structural_tag(key_string: str) → BaseGrammarObject

Caching:
- get_cached_or_future_value(key: Tuple[str, str]) → BaseGrammarObject
  Lazy compilation with ThreadPoolExecutor, returns Future if not cached
```

### 4. User-Facing API

**Location:** `/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (lines 75-139)

```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    regex: Optional[str] = None,           # Constraint parameter
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,     # Constraint parameter
    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,   # Special case (routing to SglSelect)
    # ... other sampling parameters
):
```

Also in `/python/sglang/lang/ir.py`:
- **`SglGen`** class (lines 451-504) - IR node for generation with constraints
- **`SglSamplingParams`** dataclass (lines 18-41) - Stores regex and json_schema

### 5. Constraint Registration & Creation

**Registration system** (base_grammar_backend.py, lines 199-218):
```python
GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}

def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func

def create_grammar_backend(server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids):
    # Routes to appropriate backend (outlines, xgrammar, llguidance, or none)
    # Also wraps with ReasonerGrammarBackend if reasoning mode enabled
```

**Flow:**
1. User specifies `--grammar-backend` flag (e.g., "xgrammar")
2. Server initializes grammar backend in `GrammarManager.__init__`
3. Requests with constraints go through `GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar`
4. Backend dispatches based on constraint type (json/regex/ebnf/structural_tag)

### 6. Constraint Integration in Generation Pipeline

**Location:** `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py` (lines 197-249)

**Batched constraint handling:**
```python
def update_regex_vocab_mask():
    # Create shared vocab mask (all grammars allocate same size)
    vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(...)
    
    # Fill mask for each request's current state
    for i, grammar in enumerate(grammars):
        if grammar and not grammar.finished:
            grammar.fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, i)
    
def apply_logits_bias(logits: Tensor):
    if vocab_mask is not None:
        # Apply constraint mask to logits BEFORE sampling
        apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=vocab_mask)
```

**Token acceptance flow:**
1. Token sampled from masked logits
2. Grammar accepts token: `grammar.accept_token(token_id)`
3. Grammar state advances to next state
4. Next iteration: new mask generated from updated state

### 7. Backend Implementations

**XGrammar Backend** (xgrammar_backend.py):
- Uses `xgrammar` library (C++/Rust implementation)
- Compiles to NFA/DFA
- Fast token masking with bitmasks
- Device-aware (CUDA/CPU/NPU/XPU)

Key class: **`XGrammarGrammar`** (lines 53-167)
```python
def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask, idx):
    self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)
    
def apply_vocab_mask(self, logits, vocab_mask):
    apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, vocab_mask)
```

**Outlines Backend** (outlines_backend.py):
- Uses `outlines` library FSM
- Built on interegular for regex compilation
- Slower than XGrammar but supports more backends

Key class: **`OutlinesGrammar`** (lines 42-112)

**LLGuidance Backend** (llguidance_backend.py):
- Uses `llguidance` library
- Supports structural tags with custom XML-like syntax
- Separate token bitmask allocation

### 8. Constraint Request Flow

**`GrammarManager`** (grammar_manager.py):

1. **`process_req_with_grammar(req)`** (lines 67-105):
   - Detects constraint type in sampling_params
   - Creates cache key: `(constraint_type, constraint_string)`
   - Gets grammar object (cached or future)
   - Adds to grammar_queue if compilation needed

2. **`get_ready_grammar_requests()`** (lines 107-195):
   - Polls for completed grammar compilations
   - Synchronizes across distributed ranks
   - Handles timeouts
   - Merges ready requests back to main scheduler

### 9. Constraint Type Handling

**JSON Schema:**
- Converted to regex via backend (e.g., outlines' `build_regex_from_schema`)
- Both XGrammar and Outlines support JSON directly: `compile_json_schema()`

**Regex:**
- Direct compilation: `compile_regex(pattern_string)`
- State machine guides valid next tokens

**EBNF:**
- Grammar rule compilation: `compile_grammar(ebnf_string)`
- Full grammar syntax support

**Structural Tags (Legacy):**
- XML-like: `<begin>schema</begin>...<end>schema</end>`
- Allows multiple labeled sections with schemas
- Wrapped in JSON trigger structure

**Choices (Special):**
- Not grammar-based; routed to `SglSelect` (ir.py lines 533-549)
- Uses logprob-based selection: `ChoicesSamplingMethod` (choices.py)
- Methods: token_length_normalized, greedy_token_selection, unconditional_likelihood_normalized

### 10. Constraint Validation

**Sampling parameters validation** (sampling_params.py, lines 155-161):
```python
grammars = [self.json_schema, self.regex, self.ebnf]
if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
    raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
```

Mutual exclusivity enforced: only one constraint type per generation request.

### 11. State Management

**Grammar state includes:**
- Current FSM state
- Accepted tokens list (for debugging)
- Finished flag (constraint satisfied)
- Grammar stats (compilation time, cache hit)
- Rollback capability (for speculative decoding)

**Per-request state tracking:**
- Each request has independent grammar object copy
- Parallel generation tracks separate states
- Speculative decoding can rollback N tokens

### 12. Performance Optimizations

1. **Caching:** Compiled grammars cached by (type, string) key
2. **Jump-forward:** Skip single tokens for known sequences
3. **Batch masking:** Single vocab_mask tensor for entire batch
4. **Lazy compilation:** Threaded compilation doesn't block scheduling
5. **Copy-on-use:** Grammar objects copied only when needed (parallel generation)

### 13. Test Files

Key test/example files:
- `/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py` - JSON schema constraint tests
- `/python/sglang/test/kits/json_constrained_kit.py` - Integration test mixin
- `/python/sglang/test/kits/regex_constrained_kit.py` - Regex constraint tests
- `/python/sglang/test/kits/ebnf_constrained_kit.py` - EBNF constraint tests

### Summary

SGLang's constraint system is a **pluggable, backend-agnostic architecture** where:
1. Constraints are compiled to state machines by swappable backends
2. Generated tokens are masked based on current constraint state
3. Request-level parallelism supported through state copying
4. Compilation is lazy and cached for performance
5. Reasoning models get wrapped constraint support automatically
6. Clear separation between API layer (gen/select), IR (SglGen/SglSelect), and runtime (GrammarManager/GrammarBackend)

14	"""The baseclass of a backend for grammar-guided constrained decoding."""
15	
16	import logging
17	import time
18	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
19	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
20	from threading import Event
21	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
22	
23	import torch
24	
25	from sglang.srt.server_args import ServerArgs
26	
27	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
28	
29	
30	@dataclass
31	class GrammarStats:
32	    compilation_time: Optional[float] = None
33	    schema_count: Optional[int] = None
34	    ebnf_size: Optional[int] = None
35	    is_cache_hit: bool = False
36	    is_grammar_aborted: bool = False
37	    tree_traversal_time: List[float] = field(default_factory=list)
38	    dispatch_type: Optional[str] = None
39	    num_timeout: int = 0
40	
41	
42	class BaseGrammarObject:
43	
44	    def __init__(self):
45	        self._finished = False
46	        self.grammar_stats = None
47	        self.current_token = None
48	
49	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
50	        pass
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
53	        """
54	        Accept a token in the grammar.
55	        """
56	        raise NotImplementedError()
57	
58	    def rollback(self, k: int):
59	        raise NotImplementedError()
60	
61	    def is_terminated(self):
62	        return False
63	
64	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
65	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
66	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
67	        raise NotImplementedError()
68	
69	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
70	        raise NotImplementedError()
71	
72	    @staticmethod
73	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
74	        raise NotImplementedError()
75	
76	    @staticmethod
77	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
78	        raise NotImplementedError()
79	
80	    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":
81	        return self
82	
83	    @property
84	    def finished(self):
85	        return self._finished
86	
87	    @finished.setter
88	    def finished(self, finished):
89	        self._finished = finished
90	
91	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
92	        """
93	        Try to jump forward in the grammar.
94	
95	        Returns:
96	            A jump forward helper which may be used in `jump_forward_str_state`.
97	            None if the jump forward is not possible.
98	        """
99	        raise NotImplementedError()
100	
101	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
102	        """
103	        Jump forward for the grammar.
104	
105	        Returns:
106	            A tuple of the jump forward string and the next state of the grammar
107	            (which can be used in `jump_and_retokenize` if needed).
108	        """
109	        raise NotImplementedError()
110	
111	    def jump_and_retokenize(
112	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
113	    ) -> None:
114	        """
115	        Jump forward occurs, and update the grammar state if needed.
116	        """
117	        raise NotImplementedError()
118	
119	
120	INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ = BaseGrammarObject()
121	
122	
123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197	
198	
199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend
275

1	"""Public APIs of the language."""
2	
3	import re
4	from typing import Callable, List, Optional, Union
5	
6	from sglang.global_config import global_config
7	from sglang.lang.backend.base_backend import BaseBackend
8	from sglang.lang.choices import ChoicesSamplingMethod, token_length_normalized
9	from sglang.lang.ir import (
10	    SglExpr,
11	    SglExprList,
12	    SglFunction,
13	    SglGen,
14	    SglImage,
15	    SglRoleBegin,
16	    SglRoleEnd,
17	    SglSelect,
18	    SglSeparateReasoning,
19	    SglVideo,
20	)
21	
22	
23	def function(
24	    func: Optional[Callable] = None, num_api_spec_tokens: Optional[int] = None
25	):
26	    if func:
27	        return SglFunction(func, num_api_spec_tokens=num_api_spec_tokens)
28	
29	    def decorator(func):
30	        return SglFunction(func, num_api_spec_tokens=num_api_spec_tokens)
31	
32	    return decorator
33	
34	
35	def Runtime(*args, **kwargs):
36	    # Avoid importing unnecessary dependency
37	    from sglang.lang.backend.runtime_endpoint import Runtime
38	
39	    return Runtime(*args, **kwargs)
40	
41	
42	def Engine(*args, **kwargs):
43	    # Avoid importing unnecessary dependency
44	    from sglang.srt.entrypoints.engine import Engine
45	
46	    return Engine(*args, **kwargs)
47	
48	
49	def set_default_backend(backend: BaseBackend):
50	    global_config.default_backend = backend
51	
52	
53	def flush_cache(backend: Optional[BaseBackend] = None):
54	    backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
55	    if backend is None:
56	        return False
57	
58	    # If backend is Runtime
59	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
60	        backend = backend.endpoint
61	    return backend.flush_cache()
62	
63	
64	def get_server_info(backend: Optional[BaseBackend] = None):
65	    backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
66	    if backend is None:
67	        return None
68	
69	    # If backend is Runtime
70	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
71	        backend = backend.endpoint
72	    return backend.get_server_info()
73	
74	
75	def gen(
76	    name: Optional[str] = None,
77	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
78	    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
79	    n: Optional[int] = None,
80	    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
81	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
82	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
83	    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
84	    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
85	    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
86	    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
87	    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
88	    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
89	    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
90	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
91	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
92	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
93	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
94	    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
95	    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
96	    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
97	    regex: Optional[str] = None,
98	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
99	):
100	    """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""
101	
102	    if choices:
103	        return SglSelect(
104	            name,
105	            choices,
106	            0.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
107	            token_length_normalized if choices_method is None else choices_method,
108	        )
109	
110	    # check regex is valid
111	    if regex is not None:
112	        try:
113	            re.compile(regex)
114	        except re.error as e:
115	            raise e
116	
117	    return SglGen(
118	        name,
119	        max_tokens,
120	        min_tokens,
121	        n,
122	        stop,
123	        stop_token_ids,
124	        stop_regex,
125	        temperature,
126	        top_p,
127	        top_k,
128	        min_p,
129	        frequency_penalty,
130	        presence_penalty,
131	        ignore_eos,
132	        return_logprob,
133	        logprob_start_len,
134	        top_logprobs_num,
135	        return_text_in_logprobs,
136	        dtype,
137	        regex,
138	        json_schema,
139	    )
140	
141	
142	def gen_int(
143	    name: Optional[str] = None,
144	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
145	    n: Optional[int] = None,
146	    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
147	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
148	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
149	    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
150	    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
151	    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
152	    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
153	    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
154	    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
155	    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
156	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
157	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
158	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
159	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
160	):
161	    return SglGen(
162	        name,
163	        max_tokens,
164	        None,
165	        n,
166	        stop,
167	        stop_token_ids,
168	        stop_regex,
169	        temperature,
170	        top_p,
171	        top_k,
172	        min_p,
173	        frequency_penalty,
174	        presence_penalty,
175	        ignore_eos,
176	        return_logprob,
177	        logprob_start_len,
178	        top_logprobs_num,
179	        return_text_in_logprobs,
180	        int,
181	        None,
182	    )
183	
184	
185	def gen_string(
186	    name: Optional[str] = None,
187	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
188	    n: Optional[int] = None,
189	    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
190	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
191	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
192	    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
193	    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
194	    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
195	    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
196	    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
197	    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
198	    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
199	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
200	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
201	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
202	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
203	):
204	    return SglGen(
205	        name,
206	        max_tokens,
207	        None,
208	        n,
209	        stop,
210	        stop_token_ids,
211	        stop_regex,
212	        temperature,
213	        top_p,
214	        top_k,
215	        min_p,
216	        frequency_penalty,
217	        presence_penalty,
218	        ignore_eos,
219	        return_logprob,
220	        logprob_start_len,
221	        top_logprobs_num,
222	        return_text_in_logprobs,

226	
227	
228	def image(expr: SglExpr):
229	    return SglImage(expr)
230	
231	
232	def video(path: str, num_frames: int):
233	    return SglVideo(path, num_frames)
234	
235	
236	def select(
237	    name: Optional[str] = None,
238	    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
239	    temperature: float = 0.0,
240	    choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod = token_length_normalized,
241	):
242	    assert choices is not None
243	    return SglSelect(name, choices, temperature, choices_method)
244	
245	
246	def _role_common(name: str, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
247	    if expr is None:
248	        return SglExprList([SglRoleBegin(name), SglRoleEnd(name)])
249	    else:
250	        return SglExprList([SglRoleBegin(name), expr, SglRoleEnd(name)])
251	
252	
253	def system(expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
254	    return _role_common("system", expr)
255	
256	
257	def user(expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
258	    return _role_common("user", expr)
259	
260	
261	def assistant(expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
262	    return _role_common("assistant", expr)
263	
264	
265	def system_begin():
266	    return SglRoleBegin("system")
267	
268	
269	def system_end():
270	    return SglRoleEnd("system")
271	
272	
273	def user_begin():
274	    return SglRoleBegin("user")
275	
276

278	    return SglRoleEnd("user")
279

282	    return SglRoleBegin("assistant")
283	
284

286	    return SglRoleEnd("assistant")
287	
288	
289	def separate_reasoning(
290	    expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None, model_type: Optional[str] = None
291	):
292	    return SglExprList([expr, SglSeparateReasoning(model_type, expr=expr)])
